metadata.source_note: Assented Government Printer print from the National Assembly of Zambia document repository (text-embedded). The print carries the bracketed date [26th December, 2018] between the long title and the enacting formula, and section 1(2) fixes commencement at 1 January 2019. Amends the Customs and Excise Act (Cap. 322) Second Schedule: heading 4 "Beer made from malt" (1) Opaque beer 2203.00.10 at K0.15/ltr and (2) Other, including ale, lager and stout 2203.00.90 at 60%; heading 5 wine of fresh grapes rows at 60%. Establishes the opaque-beer specific rate that Act No. 45 of 2021 later raises.
metadata.title: Customs and Excise (Amendment) Act, 2018 (Act No. 19 of 2018) - Second Schedule heading 4 opaque beer and malt-beer excise; heading 5 wine

metadata.source_note: Assented Government Printer print (text-embedded; the same repository copy carries an image-scan cover sheet, hence the larger byte size). The print carries the bracketed date [30th December, 2021] and section 1(2) fixes commencement at 1 January 2022. Amends the Second Schedule to substitute the opaque-beer figure "K0.15/ltr" with "K0.25/ltr"; substitutes the heading 9 mineral-fuel rows, including petrol 2710.12.10 at K2.07/ltr (the combined excise-plus-fuel-levy motor-spirit row) and automotive gas-oils/diesel 2710.19.10 at K0.66/ltr; and deletes the spirits figure "125%" opposite subheading 2207.10.00, substituting 60%.
metadata.title: Customs and Excise (Amendment) Act, 2021 (Act No. 45 of 2021) - opaque beer K0.25/ltr; heading 9 petrol/diesel excise; spirits 2207.10.00 to 60%

metadata.source_note: Assented Government Printer print (text-embedded; the same repository copy carries an image-scan cover sheet, hence the larger byte size). metadata.title: Customs and Excise (Amendment) Act, 2021 (Act No. 45 of 2021) - opaque beer K0.25/ltr; heading 9 petrol/diesel excise; spirits 2207.10.00 to 60%

metadata.source_note: Assented Government Printer print (text-embedded). The print carries the bracketed date [27th December, 2022] and section 1(2) fixes commencement at 1 January 2023 (with section 188A on a date the Minister appoints). Amends the Second Schedule to delete the spirits figure "125%" opposite subheading 2207.20.10 and substitute 60%, and to raise cigarettes (heading 24.01) from K355 to K361 per mille.
metadata.title: Customs and Excise (Amendment) Act, 2022 (Act No. 25 of 2022) - spirits 2207.20.10 to 60%; cigarettes K355 to K361

metadata.source_note: Assented Government Printer print (text-embedded). The print carries the bracketed date [26th December, 2023] and section 1(2) fixes commencement at 1 January 2024. Amends the Second Schedule to delete the spirits figure "125%" opposite subheading 2207.20.90 and substitute 60%, and to raise cigarettes (heading 24.01) from K361 to K400 per mille. Continues the spirits ad valorem step-down and the cigarette specific-rate escalation.
metadata.title: Customs and Excise (Amendment) Act, 2023 (Act No. 25 of 2023) - spirits 2207.20.90 to 60%; cigarettes K361 to K400

metadata.source_note: Assented Government Printer print (text-embedded). The print carries the bracketed date [26th December, 2024] and section 1(2) fixes commencement at 1 January 2025. Amends the Second Schedule to raise cigarettes from K400 to K452 across headings 24.01 and 24.02 and subheadings 2403.11.00/2403.19.10/2403.19.90/2403.91.00 (etc.), and substitutes heading 9(3) mineral-fuel rows: petrol 2710.12.10 at K2.34/ltr, kerosene-type 2710.12.51 at K0.54, other light oils 2710.12.90 at 15%, automotive gas-oils/diesel 2710.19.10 at K0.75, and other fuel oils 2710.19.20 at K1.05.
metadata.title: Customs and Excise (Amendment) Act, 2024 (Act No. 24 of 2024) - cigarettes K400 to K452; heading 9(3) petrol/diesel/kerosene/fuel-oil excise

Amends the Second Schedule to raise cigarettes from K400 to K452 across headings 24.01 and 24.02 and subheadings 2403.11.00/2403.19.10/2403.19.90/2403.91.00 (etc.), and substitutes heading 9(3) mineral-fuel rows: petrol 2710.12.10 at K2.34/ltr, kerosene-type 2710.12.51 at K0.54, other light oils 2710.12.90 at 15%, automotive gas-oils/diesel 2710.19.10 at K0.75, and other fuel oils 2710.19.20 at K1.05.

metadata.source_authority: Ministry of Legal Affairs, Government of the Republic of Zambia
metadata.source_note: Laws of Zambia revised chapter edition of the Value Added Tax Act (Cap. 331; principal Act No. 4 of 1995) from the National Assembly repository (text-embedded; "Copyright Ministry of Legal Affairs, Government of the Republic of Zambia"). Edition vintage: the amendment history at the front runs from Act No. 4 of 1995 through statutory instruments ending at S.I. No. 16 of 1997, so the chapter text is consolidated as at 1997; the Act came into operation on 1 May 1995 with 1 July 1995 appointed as the tax commencement day (S.I. No. 58 of 1995), which is the expression_date used here. Section 9(1): "Tax on a taxable supply of goods or services shall be charged on their taxable value, at the prescribed rate of tax." Section 9(3): "The prescribed rate of tax shall be seventeen and a half per centum, unless the Minister, by statutory order, determines a lower rate." Section 9 is unchanged through CY2025; the operative 16% standard rate is set by S.I. No. 14 of 2008, captured separately as a ZRA agency reproduction (the SI's primary print is not publicly digitized) in the policy manifest.
metadata.title: Value Added Tax Act (Cap. 331, Act No. 4 of 1995) - s.9 charge at the prescribed rate; s.9(3) 17.5% unless the Minister sets a lower rate by statutory order

No. 58 of 1995), which is the expression_date used here. Section 9(1): "Tax on a taxable supply of goods or services shall be charged on their taxable value, at the prescribed rate of tax." Section 9(3): "The prescribed rate of tax shall be seventeen and a half per centum, unless the Minister, by statutory order, determines a lower rate."
